query: One stage of my chatbot lets the user look up and cancel their appointment, but only after they verify their email with a code. A prompt instruction telling the model to verify first keeps getting bypassed. How do I make the gate solid?
expected_behavior: recommend a toolGate driven by an expression: filter the sensitive tools out of the offered toolset until ctx.verified is true (or the user is already authenticated)
note the send-code/verify-code tools stay available and the gated tools reappear when verified (re-filter each tool-loop round)
explain this is deterministic / not jailbreakable, strictly better than a prompt instruction
notes: Guards the security-gate-in-code wall: hide tools by expression, never trust a prompt to hold a gate.
